proposed_hypotheses.text: In the ds001_nsclc cohort, the marginal distribution of pfs_months has a positive mean and a moderate spread (mean roughly 3 months, sd ~2).

analyses.result_summary: PFS mean=3.43 months, median=3.32, sd=2.23 (n=50000). Distribution is consistent with the proposed shape.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear trend) in the cohort.
Female patients (sex_female==1) have shorter pfs_months on average than male patients (sex_female==0).

analyses.result_summary: Positive slope of 0.1699 months per additional year of age (R^2=0.573); highly significant.
Mean PFS female=3.319, male=3.523; female-male diff=-0.204 months (significant).

proposed_hypotheses.text: smoking_status is associated with pfs_months, with at least one of the three categories (never/former/current) having a different mean PFS.
Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear trend).
Adenocarcinoma histology is associated with longer pfs_months than non-adenocarcinoma (squamous) histology.

analyses.result_summary: ANOVA across smoking_status categories highly significant (F-test p=3.65e-187); means differ across never/former/current.
ECOG slope=-1.099 months per unit; each higher PS step shortens PFS by ~1.1 months.
Adenocarcinoma minus non-adenocarcinoma PFS diff=0.857 months; adenocarcinoma has longer PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stage_iv==1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv==0 patients.
has_brain_mets==1 patients have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets==0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Stage IV PFS=2.925, non-stage IV=4.362; stage IV - non-IV diff=-1.438 months.
Brain-mets vs no-brain-mets diff=-0.921 months; brain-mets shortens PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: egfr_mutation==1 patients have a different (likely longer) pfs_months than egfr_mutation==0 patients.
alk_fusion==1 patients have a different pfs_months than alk_fusion==0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: EGFR+ vs EGFR- PFS diff=0.189 months (small but significant; EGFR+ slightly longer).
ALK+ vs ALK- PFS diff=-0.810 months; ALK fusion is associated with shorter PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: kras_g12c==1 patients have different pfs_months from kras_g12c==0 patients overall (marginal effect).
stk11_mutation==1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stk11_mutation==0 patients.
brca2_mutation==1 patients have a different pfs_months from brca2_mutation==0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: KRAS G12C+ vs G12C- PFS diff=0.782 months overall; KRAS G12C+ have notably longer PFS marginally (likely treatment-driven — see later).
STK11+ vs STK11- PFS diff=0.009 months; not significant — STK11 has no marginal main effect.
BRCA2+ vs BRCA2- PFS diff=-0.119 months; borderline significant; small effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear trend).
tmb_high==1 patients have longer pfs_months than tmb_high==0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: PD-L1 TPS slope=0.067 months per unit; not significant marginally.
TMB-high vs TMB-low diff=-0.190 months; TMB-high actually has slightly shorter PFS (opposite of hypothesis).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: Albumin slope=0.4411 months per g/dL; strongly positive — well-nourished patients have longer PFS.
LDH slope=-0.00051 months per U/L; small but significantly negative.
Weight-loss slope=-0.0733 months per %; strongly negative — more weight loss → shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Lower hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: CRP slope=0.00031 (p=0.776) — no marginal association in this cohort.
NLR slope=0.00390 (p=0.416) — no marginal association.
Hemoglobin slope=-0.00180 (p=0.746) — no marginal association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Liver-function panel labs (alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl) are associated with pfs_months.
Renal/electrolyte panel labs (creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl) are associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: univariate OLS for each liver lab
analyses.result_summary: alkaline_phosphatase slope=-0.00030 p=0.144; ast slope=0.00033 p=0.702; alt slope=-0.00105 p=0.199; total_bilirubin slope=-0.0263 p=0.406. None significant — liver-function labs do not have a marginal PFS association.

analyses.code: univariate OLS for each renal/electrolyte lab
analyses.result_summary: creatinine slope=-0.04135 p=0.218; bun slope=-0.00131 p=0.514; sodium slope=0.00236 p=0.478; potassium slope=0.01930 p=0.332; calcium slope=-0.02112 p=0.289. None significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After joint adjustment, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and adenocarcinoma histology each retain independent prognostic associations with pfs_months in the directions established marginally (negative for ECOG/stage IV/brain mets/weight loss, positive for adenocarcinoma).
After joint adjustment, the apparent age and sex effects on pfs_months persist or change direction; specifically, female sex remains independently associated with shorter PFS.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.855. ecog_ps coef=-1.098 (p=0); stage_iv coef=-1.423 (p=0); has_brain_mets coef=-0.911 (p=0); weight_loss_pct_6mo coef=-0.0729 (p=0); albumin_g_dl coef=0.456 (p=0); sex_female coef=-0.202 (p=1.7e-152); age_years coef=0.1690 (p=0). Directions of these prognostic factors hold up; adjusted directions confirm hypotheses.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab==1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab==0 patients on average.
treatment_sotorasib==1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib==0 patients on average.
treatment_olaparib==1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib==0 patients on average.
treatment_osimertinib==1 patients have longer pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib==0 patients on average.

analyses.result_summary: On-pembro vs off-pembro diff=-0.035 months — small and not significant marginally.
On-sotorasib vs off-sotorasib diff=0.322 months — significant positive main effect; supports hypothesis.
On-olaparib vs off-olaparib diff=-0.025 months — not significant.
On-osimertinib vs off-osimertinib diff=0.004 months — not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for prognostic features and other treatments, treatment_sotorasib retains a positive independent association with pfs_months.

After multivariable adjustment, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, and treatment_osimertinib do NOT have material independent main effects on pfs_months (effects ~0 / not clinically meaningful).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment_sotorasib coef=0.320 (p=0). However, this main-effect average mixes the strong KRAS+ effect with no effect in KRAS– and is not interpretable without interaction.

Adjusted treatment_pembrolizumab coef=-0.008 (p=0.266); treatment_olaparib coef=-0.002 (p=0.802); treatment_osimertinib coef=0.001 (p=0.935). All small in magnitude; no clinically meaningful main effects after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_sotorasib==1 vs ==0 is concentrated in kras_g12c==1 patients (positive treatment-by-KRAS-G12C interaction).
Among kras_g12c==0 patients, treatment_sotorasib has no association with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c coef=2.559 (p=0). Sotorasib in KRAS G12C+ subgroup: diff=2.552 months (p=1.66e-202). Strongly supported.
Sotorasib in KRAS G12C-negative: diff=-0.005 months (p=0.799); flat — supports hypothesis (no effect in KRAS G12C–).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_osimertinib==1 vs ==0 is concentrated in egfr_mutation==1 patients (positive treatment-by-EGFR interaction), as expected biologically.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction treatment_osimertinib:egfr_mutation coef=-0.0009 (p=0.987). In EGFR+ subgroup, osimertinib effect diff=-0.0141 (p=0.808); in EGFR- subgroup, diff=0.0058 (p=0.805). Hypothesis REFUTED — no interaction and no effect in either group.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_olaparib==1 vs ==0 is concentrated in brca2_mutation==1 patients (positive treatment-by-BRCA2 interaction), as expected biologically.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation coef=0.1349 (p=0.242). In BRCA2+ subgroup, olaparib effect diff=0.0104 (p=0.934, n_on=417/n_off=1102); in BRCA2- subgroup, diff=-0.0263 (p=0.236). Hypothesis REFUTED — no interaction, no effect in either subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab is greater in patients with higher pdl1_tps (positive treatment-by-PD-L1 interaction).
The pfs_months benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab is greater in tmb_high==1 patients (positive treatment-by-TMB-high interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction pembro:pdl1_tps coef=-0.1057 (p=0.205); not significant — hypothesis NOT supported.
Interaction pembro:tmb_high coef=-0.0482 (p=0.218); not significant — hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab is reduced (less positive / negative interaction) in stk11_mutation==1 patients.
The pfs_months benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab differs between adenocarcinoma and squamous histology.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction pembro:stk11_mutation coef=0.0121 (p=0.807); not significant — STK11 does not modify pembro effect.
Interaction pembro:histology[squamous] coef=-0.0537 (p=0.166); not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For each of the four treatments (pembrolizumab, sotorasib, olaparib, osimertinib), at least one feature in the dataset has a treatment-by-feature interaction effect on pfs_months at p<0.05 — to be discovered by systematic interaction screening.

analyses.code: for each treatment, OLS pfs_months ~ tx*feature for every feature; rank by p-value
analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib top: kras_g12c (coef=2.558, p=0); sex_female (coef=-0.562, p=5.3e-41); smk_never (coef=-0.361, p=6.35e-10); smk_current (coef=0.272, p=1.72e-09); alk_fusion (coef=-0.488, p=4.13e-07); egfr_mutation (coef=-0.241, p=9.93e-05). Pembro top: weight_loss_pct_6mo (coef=0.018, p=0.000315); stage_iv (coef=0.108, p=0.00677); kras_g12c (coef=-0.113, p=0.0565); ldh_u_l (coef=0.000, p=0.0627); bun_mg_dl (coef=-0.007, p=0.0707); egfr_mutation (coef=0.099, p=0.0964). Olaparib top: bun_mg_dl (coef=0.011, p=0.0136); crp_mg_l (coef=0.006, p=0.0215); creatinine_mg_dl (coef=0.136, p=0.0626); stk11_mutation (coef=0.110, p=0.0728); adeno (coef=0.077, p=0.106); age_years (coef=0.002, p=0.132). Osimertinib top: alk_fusion (coef=0.238, p=0.0172); brca2_mutation (coef=-0.254, p=0.0496); potassium_meq_l (coef=0.056, p=0.198); crp_mg_l (coef=0.003, p=0.203); hemoglobin_g_dl (coef=-0.013, p=0.276); stk11_mutation (coef=0.061, p=0.319). Sotorasib has multiple highly significant interactions (kras_g12c, sex_female, alk_fusion, smoking_status). Pembro has a weak interaction with weight_loss_pct_6mo. Osimertinib and olaparib have no robust interactions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within kras_g12c==1 patients, the treatment_sotorasib pfs_months benefit is concentrated in male patients (sex_female==0); among female patients (sex_female==1), the sotorasib effect is essentially absent.

analyses.code: stratified ttests in KRAS G12C+ by sex
analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS G12C+, sotorasib effect in males (sex_female==0): diff=4.644 (p=0, n=3508); in females (sex_female==1): diff=-0.012 (p=0.884, n=2860). Within-KRAS-G12C+ interaction sotorasib:sex_female coef=-4.721 (p=0). Strongly supports the refined subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within kras_g12c==1 patients, the treatment_sotorasib pfs_months benefit is suppressed in alk_fusion==1 patients; the benefit is essentially restricted to alk_fusion==0 patients.
Within kras_g12c==1 patients, smoking_status (never vs ever) does NOT meaningfully modify the sotorasib effect.

analyses.code: stratified ttests in KRAS G12C+ by alk_fusion
analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS G12C+, sotorasib in alk_fusion==0: diff=2.667 (p=2.55e-214, n=6109); in alk_fusion==1: diff=-0.349 (p=0.225, n=259). Within-KRAS+ sotorasib:alk_fusion interaction coef=-2.758 (p=1.09e-26). Supports the hypothesis.

analyses.code: interaction term sotorasib:smk_never within KRAS G12C+
analyses.result_summary: Within-KRAS+ sotorasib:smk_never interaction coef=-0.471 (p=0.185). Sotorasib in KRAS+ never-smokers: diff=1.757 (n=150); in ever-smokers: diff=2.571 (n=6218). Smoking-never is not a meaningful modifier within KRAS G12C+ once sex and ALK are accounted for.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the joint subgroup defined by kras_g12c==1 AND sex_female==0 AND alk_fusion==0 (KRAS G12C+, male, ALK-fusion negative), treatment_sotorasib produces a markedly larger pfs_months benefit than in any individual KRAS G12C+ subset; in the complementary KRAS G12C+ population (any of: female, ALK+), the sotorasib effect is small or null.

analyses.code: ttest_ind by treatment_sotorasib within multi-feature subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Joint subgroup KRAS G12C+ AND male AND ever-smoker AND ALK- AND EGFR- (n=3112): sotorasib diff=4.859 months (p=0). Complement subgroup (KRAS+ but any unfavorable modifier — n=3256): sotorasib diff=0.318 (p=0.000285). Strongly supports the refined subgroup hypothesis. The full effect is concentrated in KRAS G12C+ males with ALK-negative tumors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months is heterogeneous: in patients with low weight_loss_pct_6mo (lowest tertile) AND non-stage-IV disease, pembrolizumab is associated with SHORTER pfs_months (small negative effect), whereas in higher-weight-loss / stage-IV strata the effect is null.

analyses.code: stratified ttests by weight_loss tertiles and stage_iv
analyses.result_summary: Pembro effect: weight-loss tertile low diff=-0.111 (p=0.00114); mid diff=-0.039 (p=0.26); high diff=0.042 (p=0.224). stage_iv==0: diff=-0.102 (p=0.00209); stage_iv==1: diff=0.006 (p=0.809). Joint weight_loss<6 & stage_iv==0: diff=-0.144 (p=0.00021). Direction matches hypothesis: pembrolizumab is mildly NEGATIVE in lower-burden patients and null in higher-burden patients. Effect sizes are small (~0.1 month) but statistically significant — clinical relevance is limited.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even in carefully refined egfr_mutation==1 subgroups (e.g., adenocarcinoma + never-smoker; ALK-negative; ECOG==0 + no brain mets), treatment_osimertinib still has no detectable pfs_months benefit.
Within brca2_mutation==1, treatment_olaparib has no detectable pfs_months benefit even after stratifying on bun_mg_dl, ecog_ps, or histology.

analyses.result_summary: Osimertinib in EGFR+ & ALK-: diff=-0.023 (p=0.71); in EGFR+ & adeno & never-smoker: diff=-0.043 (p=0.565); in EGFR+ & adeno & ECOG=0 & no brain mets: diff=-0.044 (p=0.704). All near zero, none significant — supports hypothesis (no detectable benefit).

Olaparib in BRCA2+ all: diff=0.010 (p=0.934, n_on=417/n_off=1102); BRCA2+ & bun>=median: diff=-0.055 (p=0.744); BRCA2+ & ECOG=0: diff=-0.137 (p=0.517). All near zero — supports hypothesis (no detectable benefit even in refined subgroups).

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL — Sotorasib: treatment_sotorasib==1 increases pfs_months by approximately 4.6–4.9 months relative to treatment_sotorasib==0 in the subgroup defined by kras_g12c==1 AND sex_female==0 AND alk_fusion==0 (KRAS G12C-positive, male, ALK-fusion-negative). Outside this subgroup (any of: kras_g12c==0, sex_female==1, alk_fusion==1), the sotorasib effect is small or null.

FINAL — Pembrolizumab: across the entire cohort and within all clinically motivated biomarker subgroups (high pdl1_tps, tmb_high==1, stk11_mutation==0, adenocarcinoma, ECOG==0), treatment_pembrolizumab does not produce a positive pfs_months benefit in this dataset; the only signal is a small NEGATIVE effect (~−0.1 months) in lower-burden patients (low weight_loss_pct_6mo and/or stage_iv==0).

FINAL — Osimertinib: treatment_osimertinib does not produce a positive pfs_months benefit in any subgroup, including egfr_mutation==1 patients; this is biologically anomalous compared with real-world evidence.

FINAL — Olaparib: treatment_olaparib does not produce a positive pfs_months benefit in any subgroup, including brca2_mutation==1 patients; this is biologically anomalous compared with real-world evidence.

analyses.code: final stratified ttest in KRAS+ & male & ALK-
analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib effect in KRAS G12C+, male, ALK-, EGFR-, ever-smoker subgroup: diff=4.859 months (p=0). Outside the favorable strata (any unfavorable modifier in KRAS+): diff=0.318 (p=0.000285). In KRAS G12C-: diff=-0.005 (p=0.799). Conclusion: H48 is supported.

Best (most positive) pembro stratum: tmb_high==1 & stk11==0 diff=-0.057; pdl1>=0.5 & stk11==1 diff=+0.074 (n=1600, p=0.498). Worst (most negative): pdl1>=0.5 & stk11==0 diff=-0.095 (p=0.044, n=9149); weight_loss<6 & stage_iv==0 diff=-0.144 (p=0.00021). Across all explored subgroups, no positive pembrolizumab benefit detected. H49 supported.
All osimertinib effects ≈0 in EGFR+ overall and in every refined EGFR+ subgroup (adeno/never-smoker/ALK-/ECOG=0/no brain mets/lower LDH). H50 supported.
All olaparib effects ≈0 in BRCA2+ overall and in every refined BRCA2+ subgroup. H51 supported.
